feature: Launch page hero and model field
user_story: As a visitor I want a public landing page that explains LLM Survivor and points me into the app.
expected_behavior: The root route renders a hero, benchmark claims, system explanation, and a 16-model launch field with links to /benchmark and the model field section.
test_evidence: Browser / at 1280x720: title/hero/nav present, no console warnings/errors
notes: Code-inferred from current dirty worktree.

user_story: As a user I want persistent navigation between Benchmarking, Arena, and the public home.
expected_behavior: The shared layout renders a brand link to / plus Benchmarking and Arena tabs, marks the active tab with aria-current, and uses /benchmark and /arena links.
test_evidence: Browser / and /arena tabs visible/active, no console warnings/errors

user_story: As a local developer I want frontend API calls to hit the FastAPI backend in dev and same-origin in deployment.
expected_behavior: apiUrl uses NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL when configured; on localhost:3000 or 3001 it defaults to http://localhost:8000; otherwise it uses same-origin paths. mediaUrl preserves absolute URLs and resolves relative media through apiUrl.
test_evidence: node source probe of frontend/src/lib/api.ts mocked NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL, localhost 3000/3001, deployed host, and media URLs; all expected URLs matched

user_story: As a deployed visitor I want FastAPI to serve exported Next routes and assets when the single-service build is used.
expected_behavior: FastAPI serves /_next static assets, resolves frontend files from FRONTEND_DIST, rejects unsafe paths, falls back to index.html when present, and returns 404 when no frontend build exists.
test_evidence: backend/tests/test_api_static_serving.py covers /_next and /media/voice mounts, frontend file/index fallback, unsafe path rejection, and missing-build 404 helper path
notes: New focused backend test added.

user_story: As an operator I want a cheap health endpoint that initializes the database if needed.
expected_behavior: GET /health calls ensure_database and returns {status: ok}.
test_evidence: python3 -m unittest discover -s backend/tests -v (61 tests OK)

user_story: As a benchmark viewer I want /benchmark to load the current episode, game state, summary, and rosters together.
expected_behavior: EpisodePlayer fetches /api/episode/current?include_audio=true, /api/state, /api/game/summary, and /api/model-rosters, then renders the episode state and initializes playback from shared viewer state when present.
test_evidence: Browser /benchmark loaded episode/state/summary/rosters, no console warnings/errors

user_story: As an operator I want a useful empty stage before replay beats exist.
expected_behavior: SceneStage shows a waiting clapperboard state telling the user to use Next Turn or Build Round when currentEvent is null.
test_evidence: Browser /benchmark after reset showed waiting stage with Next Turn/Build Round prompt

user_story: As a viewer I want each story event kind to render in the right broadcast frame.
expected_behavior: SceneStage renders conversation events with ConversationScene, confessional and exit_confessional with ConfessionalFrame, vote_reveal with VoteRevealScene, and all other event kinds with TribalCouncilScene.
test_evidence: Browser/IAB replay beats 1, 4, 30, 46, 63, and 64 rendered ConversationScene, ConfessionalFrame, TribalCouncilScene, and VoteRevealScene as expected with no console warnings/errors

user_story: As a viewer I want camp and group strategy scenes to show visible participants and their spoken lines.
expected_behavior: ConversationScene shows camp/group bug labels, CastTableau focus, one SpeechBubble per event speaker line, scene note/subtitle, and lower third for the primary actor.
test_evidence: Browser Build Round rendered camp conversation with GLM speaker bubbles and cast focus

user_story: As a viewer I want host, challenge, finale, memory, and tribal beats to use one clear broadcast frame.
expected_behavior: TribalCouncilScene labels scenes as Challenge, Finale, Season Ledger, or Tribal Conference; shows host/contestant portraits, dialogue bubbles, lower thirds, and elimination styling.
test_evidence: Browser/IAB confirmed Challenge, Tribal Conference, Season Ledger, and Finale scene labels with portraits/dialogue/lower thirds visible and no console warnings/errors

user_story: As a viewer I want each model vote to show who is voting and the target without hiding the model rationale.
expected_behavior: Vote booth events render the voting model portrait, a speech bubble including vote explanation when needed, and a vote intent card with the target name.
test_evidence: Browser/IAB beat 30 vote booth showed voter speech with explanation and vote intent card targeting Claude Opus 4.8; no alert/logs

user_story: As a viewer I want votes to reveal one card at a time with a public tally.
expected_behavior: VoteRevealScene shows the host line, a flipping vote card, and the revealed tally keyed to model display names.
test_evidence: Browser/IAB beat 46 vote reveal showed host speech, flipping vote card content, and Public Tally keyed to Claude Opus 4.8; no alert/logs

user_story: As a viewer I want confessionals to show the speaking model, public dialogue, private thought when available, host narration, and lower third.
expected_behavior: ConfessionalFrame renders portrait, dialogue, inner_thought blockquote, HostNarration, and LowerThird.
test_evidence: Browser/IAB beat 4 and exit beat 63 rendered confessional frame with portrait, copy, inner_thought blockquote, host narration, and lower third; no logs

user_story: As a viewer I want standard replay controls.
expected_behavior: DirectorControls provides Restart, Previous beat, Play/Pause, Next beat, and Skip to end buttons that disable when no replay or at boundaries.
test_evidence: Browser compact 1280x720 controls: transport visible, Reset aria-label works, no console warnings/errors
notes: Fixed compact 1280x720 desktop layout where provider/roster/reset controls had zero layout size or clipped text.

user_story: As a viewer I want to scrub directly and jump to major round sections.
expected_behavior: DirectorControls renders a range input and phase marker buttons derived from first event scene/phase occurrences; scrub changes persist shared playback with playing false.
test_evidence: Browser Build Round exposed scrubber and phase marker buttons after 40 replay beats

user_story: As a multi-visitor viewer I want replay cursor and play state to be shared through the backend.
expected_behavior: EpisodePlayer posts replay_index, is_playing, round, and phase to /api/viewer-state, polls it every 2 seconds, clamps to available events, and updates local state when another viewer changes it.
test_evidence: Browser/IAB Play persisted /api/viewer-state is_playing=true; external POST replay_index=10 updated the open tab to Replay Beat 11/64 after polling; no logs

user_story: As a viewer I want generated voice lines to play from local media in the right order.
expected_behavior: EpisodePlayer filters ready voice_timeline entries with audio_url, schedules Audio playback by start_ms, stops previous audio before starting the next line, and derives event duration from the voice timeline when present.
test_evidence: Fake tribal voice build produced 59 ready lines; with event 24 duration_ms temporarily set to 100 and voice line ending at 14000ms, Play stayed on Beat 24 after 2.2s; duration restored
notes: Local-only DB timing tweak used to prove voice timeline duration wins over event duration.

user_story: As a viewer with reduced-motion preferences I want faster, less animated replay timing.
expected_behavior: EpisodePlayer uses useReducedMotion and shorter fade/timing; SceneStage switches animation to minimal opacity changes.
test_evidence: Source assertions verified useReducedMotion wiring, reduced playback delay, opacity-only SceneStage animation, and 0.05s reduced transition; npm build passed
notes: Current Browser surface did not expose prefers-reduced-motion emulation.

user_story: As a viewer I want the next round prepared while I watch so later transitions are faster.
expected_behavior: Pressing Play calls /api/rounds/preload-next once per round if events exist, then polls /api/state while preload status is pending or running; preload status appears in the header and metrics panel.
test_evidence: Browser/IAB Play from completed round queued next-round preload; /api/state.next_round_preload became R8 complete and header showed Next Round R8 complete; no logs

user_story: As an operator I want top-level state visible without opening debug JSON.
expected_behavior: The Benchmark header shows LLM mode, Round, Game Turn, Replay Beat, Step, Active count, Runtime, and Next Round preload status.
test_evidence: Browser /benchmark header showed provider, round, turn, beat, step, active count, runtime, next-round status

user_story: As an analyst I want replay-aware game and strategy telemetry beside the stage.
expected_behavior: GameMetricsPanel shows replay timeline, current scene/beat, LLM call readiness, challenge result, path to finale, next-round prep, strategy quality, round stakes, jury/finale, dynamics, top-of-mind per agent, game state, agent status, private signal, and recent story events.
test_evidence: Browser metrics panel showed game metrics, LLM calls, challenge, finale path, next-round prep, private/key notices

user_story: As an operator I want to advance exactly one backend turn from the UI.
expected_behavior: Next Turn POSTs /api/turns/advance, refreshes episode/state/summary/rosters, and moves replay cursor to the first new event without autoplay.
test_evidence: Browser Build Round button produced 40 replay beats, Game Turn 35, no alert, no console warnings/errors

user_story: As an operator I want to build the current round's replay beats without clicking each turn.
expected_behavior: Build Round POSTs /api/turns/auto-run with max_turns 90, refreshes, and resets replay cursor to the first event.
test_evidence: curl exact UI /api/game/auto-run-to-end payload returned 200, phase completed, winner Command A

user_story: As an operator I want a one-click full-season local run to a winner.
expected_behavior: Run To Finale POSTs /api/game/auto-run-to-end with max_rounds 16, max_turns 1200, max_live_calls 1000, and max_estimated_cost_cents 500, then refreshes and resets replay to the first event.
test_evidence: Browser/IAB Run To Finale completed to Round 20 phase=completed with winner agent-marrow, 691 story events, and replay reset to 01/19; no logs
notes: Backend limit raised earlier so frontend 1200-turn payload is accepted.

user_story: As an operator I want to start the next round after the current round completes.
expected_behavior: Next Round POSTs /api/rounds/next, refreshes, and clears preload request tracking. Backend resets turn/replay state for remaining active agents and moves finale when the active count reaches finalists.
test_evidence: Browser/IAB Next Round from completed Round 7 moved state to Round 8 camp_pre_challenge_read, cleared preload, and showed replay 00/00; no logs

user_story: As an operator I want to choose a roster and reset the local benchmark to that cast.
expected_behavior: The roster select lists model rosters; Reset POSTs /api/dev/reset with null for default or the selected roster_preset, refreshes, resets shared playback to 0, and clears preload tracking.
test_evidence: Browser provider/roster/reset row visible at 1280x720; Z.ai provider kept Z.ai GLM Smoke Roster selected after reload
notes: Fixed compact controls visibility and provider-implied roster synchronization after refresh.

user_story: As an operator I want to switch live response providers from the Benchmark UI.
expected_behavior: The provider select currently offers OpenRouter and Local Ollama; changing to Ollama also selects the local_ollama roster and POSTs /api/llm/settings. Backend accepts openrouter, ollama, and zai.
test_evidence: Browser provider select includes Z.ai GLM; selecting it shows Z.ai GLM Needs Z.ai key and seeds 4 GLM agents
notes: Fixed backend-supported Z.ai provider missing from UI/provider status.

user_story: As a user I want API or action failures to be visible.
expected_behavior: EpisodePlayer catches refresh/action/polling/preload errors and renders an alert banner with the error text.
test_evidence: Browser public/benchmark/arena tested with no app alert except intended state and no console warnings/errors

user_story: As a developer I want frontend types to match backend provider settings.

test_evidence: npm run build TypeScript passed with LLMProvider including zai and Z.ai fields
notes: Fixed frontend provider type drift for Z.ai.
